message:
ext4 MOVE_EXT: resolve donor fd via /proc, add scenario-id logs, track mountpoint

Wires up the backend half of EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT so the FUSE dispatch can
translate a caller-process donor fd into a mounted-path inode, temporarily
register the mapping with the ext4 backend, execute the move, and emit
operator-visible structured logs for both success and rejection paths.

Changes:

- crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:
  * Track the mountpoint on FuseInner so donor-fd resolution can cross-check
    that the caller's donor file lives on the same device as the mount.
  * Replace the public FrankenFuse::new / with_options constructors with a
    shared private with_inner builder; the public constructors remain as
    thin wrappers so external callers are unaffected. mount(),
    mount_background(), and mount_managed() now pass the mountpoint through
    to with_inner so runtime ioctl handling has the anchor it needs.
  * Add resolve_move_ext_donor(caller_pid, donor_fd): opens
    /proc/{pid}/fd/{donor_fd}, reads metadata, returns EBADF on failure,
    EINVAL when the donor inode's device number does not match the mount's
    device number. This closes the cross-filesystem-donor attack surface.
  * In dispatch_ioctl for MOVE_EXT: capture caller pid from the FUSE
    request, resolve the donor inode, call
    FsOps::register_move_ext_donor_fd before the move and
    unregister_move_ext_donor_fd after (on both success and failure paths).
  * Emit structured tracing events under target "ffs::ioctl" with
    operation_id (deterministic from ino+donor_fd+orig_start+donor_start+len),
    scenario_id "ext4_ioctl_move_ext", outcome (success/rejected/failed),
    error_class, and full byte offset payload. Rejection paths classify
    errno into EBADF/EINVAL/EPERM/EROFS/EOPNOTSUPP/ENOTTY as "rejected";
    all others as "failed" — matching the bd-mexte log-contract expectations.
  * Update Filesystem::ioctl to forward req.pid() into dispatch_ioctl.

- crates/ffs-core/src/vfs.rs: add two new trait hooks on FsOps with default
  implementations — register_move_ext_donor_fd (Ok(()) default) and
  unregister_move_ext_donor_fd (no-op default). Only backends that key
  donor lookup by userspace fd need to override them.

- crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs: implement both hooks for OpenFs, gated on
  FsFlavor::Ext4. Ext4 stores the mapping in a Mutex<HashMap<u32, ino>>
  keyed by donor_fd → canonical inode; btrfs takes the default no-op path.

- crates/ffs-fuse/Cargo.toml: add tracing-subscriber as a dev-dependency
  so e2e tests and harness runners can subscribe to the new ffs::ioctl
  spans without a bespoke subscriber setup.

This is the backend pair to bd-mexte (mounted-path E2E / log-contract
coverage for EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT) and completes the resolver + logging
prerequisites that the E2E bead depends on.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
